_comment: P3 Singapore Maths — misconception taxonomy. Edit this file to refine mappings as evidence accumulates.

place_value_digit_vs_value.label: Digit vs Value Confusion
place_value_digit_vs_value.description: Confuses the digit in a position with the value of that position. E.g., the digit 0 in 701 has value 0, not 10.
place_value_digit_vs_value.cpa_notes: Concrete: use place-value discs or MAB blocks to show that a 0 disc contributes nothing. Pictorial: number expanders.

place_value_relative_neighbour.label: 10 More / 10 Less Errors
place_value_relative_neighbour.description: Errors when adding or subtracting 10 (or 100). Often changes the wrong digit or changes the ones digit instead of tens.
place_value_relative_neighbour.cpa_notes: Pictorial: hundreds chart — moving one row up/down shows only the tens digit changes. Edge cases: crossing hundreds boundary.

place_value_expanded_form.label: Expanded Form Errors
place_value_expanded_form.description: Cannot decompose or recompose numbers between standard and expanded form. Often omits or doubles a place.
place_value_expanded_form.cpa_notes: Concrete: MAB blocks grouped by place. Pictorial: number expander strip.

place_value_numeral_word.label: Numeral ↔ Word Form
place_value_numeral_word.description: Errors converting between numerals and number words, especially for numbers with internal zeros.
place_value_numeral_word.cpa_notes: Worked examples with 4-digit numbers containing zeros; contrast correct vs common error.

ordering_digit_by_digit.label: Ordering / Comparison Strategy
ordering_digit_by_digit.description: Does not use systematic left-to-right digit comparison. May sort by last digit or by number of digits without aligning place values.
ordering_digit_by_digit.cpa_notes: Pictorial: number line placement; align digits in columns to make comparison visual.

number_pattern_rule.label: Pattern Rule Identification
number_pattern_rule.description: Cannot identify the additive or multiplicative rule in a number sequence, or misapplies it (e.g., adds instead of multiplies).
number_pattern_rule.cpa_notes: Pictorial: difference arrows between terms. Distinguish +/- patterns from ×/÷ patterns explicitly.

operation_selection.label: Wrong Operation Selected
operation_selection.description: Selects the wrong arithmetic operation in a word problem. Common: uses subtraction when addition is needed, or vice versa.
operation_selection.cpa_notes: Bar model is the primary Singapore tool for operation selection. Build the model before writing the equation.

multi_step_sequencing.label: Multi-Step Problem Sequencing
multi_step_sequencing.description: Individual operations are correct but performed in wrong order, or an intermediate step is skipped.
multi_step_sequencing.cpa_notes: Teach explicit step labelling: Step 1 / Step 2. Bar model helps identify sequence before computing.

fraction_denominator_size.label: Denominator-as-Count Confusion
fraction_denominator_size.description: Treats denominator as a count rather than a unit size, concluding larger denominator = larger fraction.
fraction_denominator_size.cpa_notes: Concrete: fraction strips or circles. Key image: 1/8 of a pizza is smaller than 1/3 of the same pizza — more pieces means each piece is smaller.

fraction_addition_whole_number.label: Fraction Addition Like Whole Numbers
fraction_addition_whole_number.description: Adds (or subtracts) both numerators and denominators separately: 1/2 + 1/3 = 2/5.
fraction_addition_whole_number.cpa_notes: Fraction strips: you cannot add pieces of different sizes without making them the same size first. Equivalent fractions prerequisite.

measurement_unit_conversion.label: Unit Conversion Errors
measurement_unit_conversion.description: Errors converting between metric units: cm↔m, g↔kg, mL↔L, m↔km. Often multiplies when should divide or uses wrong conversion factor.
measurement_unit_conversion.cpa_notes: Concrete: show 100 cm ruler = 1 m ruler. Table of conversion facts before practice.

money_cents_dollars.label: Cents ↔ Dollars Conversion
money_cents_dollars.description: Errors converting between cents and dollars. May treat $1.05 as 15 cents, or 250 cents as $2.5 instead of $2.50.
money_cents_dollars.cpa_notes: Concrete: physical coins/notes. Pictorial: 100-cent number bond to $1.

concrete_representation.label: Concrete Representation
concrete_representation.description: Use physical or visual manipulatives (place-value discs, fraction strips, coins, rulers) to make the abstract concept tangible.

pictorial_bar_model.label: Pictorial / Bar Model
pictorial_bar_model.description: Use the Singapore bar model or other diagrams (number line, fraction strip, hundreds chart) to represent the problem visually before computing.

worked_example_correct.label: Worked Example (Correct Method)
worked_example_correct.description: Step-by-step demonstration of the correct procedure, with explicit annotation of each decision.

worked_example_contrast.label: Worked Example (Error Contrast)
worked_example_contrast.description: Present the student's error alongside the correct method, explicitly naming what went wrong and why.

edge_case_exposure.label: Edge Case Exposure
edge_case_exposure.description: Deliberate practice on boundary cases that reveal and dislodge the misconception (e.g., crossing hundreds boundary for ±10, zero digits, unit fractions with large denominators).

retrieval_varied.label: Varied Retrieval Practice
retrieval_varied.description: Retrieval practice with varied surface features (different numbers, different wording) to build flexible, transferable knowledge.

_notes.null_abstract_type: structure_ids with abstract_type=null are pure computation errors — a wrong answer is more likely a careless slip than a misconception. Do not trigger interventions for these.
_notes.threshold: Only create an intervention if the student's accuracy on this structure_id in the triggering session is below 0.6 (i.e., got more wrong than right).
_notes.update_cadence: After every 20 completed interventions, re-rank intervention_types_ranked within each abstract_type based on observed mean post-score. Manually review any intervention with mean post_score < 0.6.
_notes.splitting_signal: If lift (post_score - pre_score) within an abstract_type is bimodal — good for some students, near-zero for others — consider splitting the abstract_type.
